ModMetaData.name: Better Trade Colors
ModMetaData.author: Kyle Givler
ModMetaData.description:
Better Trade Colors completely overhauls the visual clarity of RimWorld by universally color-coding items based on quality, durability, and condition. Instead of being restricted to a single window, these non-intrusive colors apply globally across all item text labels including trade/caravan interfaces, stockpile lists, LWM Deep Storage menus, and the bottom-left inspect panel. No more squinting or checking info cards; instantly parse your items at a glance.
Key Mechanics and Color Priorities: - Tainted Apparel (Dim Red/Brown): Instantly catch items stripped from corpses before accidentally using, or selling them. - Damaged Goods (Vibrant Magenta): Apparel under 50% durability is highlighted in sharp purple/magenta, alerting you to low hitpoint apparel. - Quality Spectrum: Beautiful, non-clashing colors that map directly to the classic RimWorld quality tiers: * Awful / Poor (Greyscale) * Normal (White) * Good (Soft Green) * Excellent (Yellow / Gold) * Masterwork (True Orange) * Legendary (Cyan / Light Blue)
Compatible with Trade UI Revised, LWM's Deep Storage, and RimHUD.
I wrote this mod because I was unhappy with the architectural mess of QualityColors. While this iteration focuses on core utility, it provides an easier to maintain, lightweight foundation for exactly what I need.
Technical Focus: - Zero Bloat: Built from scratch to avoid the heavy, outdated overhead of legacy UI color mods. (QuailtyColor) - Ultra High-Performance & Stability: Uses optimized Harmony patches to inject colors at the string factory level. Includes a custom, cache-aware Rich-Text truncator that prevents UI elements from breaking or cutting off text when color tags are applied. - Stack-Aware: Intelligently separates item names from quantity multipliers, ensuring item stack counts (e.g., "x75") remain standard UI text colors so they don't break external mod calculations or sorting algorithms. - Conflict-Free: Safely modifies string presentation layers without altering underlying data structures or game states.
Plans: - Future updates will include customizable colors via a mod menu. - Customizable HP percentages for damaged goods. - Possibly other customizations, open to suggestions
Source Code: https://github.com/JoyfulReaper/BetterTradeColors
